steps.title: Distribute using FOIL
steps.content:
Multiply each term in the first factor by each term in the second:
$$(2x^3)(x^3) + (2x^3)(-2x) + (3x)(x^3) + (3x)(-2x)$$
$$= 2x^6 - 4x^4 + 3x^4 - 6x^2$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
The only like terms are the \(x^4\) terms:
$$-4x^4 + 3x^4 = -x^4$$
Simplified result: $$2x^6 - x^4 - 6x^2$$

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• A: \(x^3 + 5x\) — Wrong degree and wrong operation; adding instead of multiplying would not give this. Incorrect.
  • B: \(3x^3 + x\) — Also wrong degree; no path from the product gives degree-3 as the leading term. Incorrect.
  • C: \(2x^6 - x^4 - 6x^2\) — Matches our simplified result exactly. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: \(2x^6 - 4x^4 + 3x^4 - 6x^2\) — This is the unsimplified intermediate step before combining \(-4x^4 + 3x^4\); it is equal to C but not in simplified form. Incorrect as the best equivalent.
